output.explanation: Most values start with the prefix "al_"; "ak_scip-card-2" deviates from the dominant prefix pattern seen in "al_scip-card-2" and many other "al_*" values.
The prevalent pattern is "al_<code>-<suffix>"; "xl_scip-inf-2" uses the rare prefix "xl_" instead of the dominant "al_" seen for the same stem.
Most values use separators like "_" and "-" in patterns such as "al_scip-inf-2"; "al_scipxinfx2" removes these separators and is malformed.
For the same stem, the dominant form is "al_scip-inf-1"; "ak_scip-inf-1" deviates by using the rare prefix "ak_".
The common stem is "scip" as in "al_scip-vte-1" and "al_scip-vte-2"; "al_scix-vte-1" has a one-character misspelling in that stem.
For the same stem, the dominant form is "al_scip-vte-1"; "ak_scip-vte-1" deviates by using the rare prefix "ak_".
For the same stem, the dominant form is "al_scip-vte-2"; "ak_scip-vte-2" deviates by using the rare prefix "ak_".
The common stem is "scip" as in "al_scip-vte-2"; "al_scix-vte-2" has a one-character misspelling in that stem.
The hf series follows numbered forms like "al_hf-1" to "al_hf-4"; "al_hf-x" breaks the numeric suffix pattern.
For the hf series, the dominant prefix is "al_" in "al_hf-1" to "al_hf-4"; "ak_hf-1" deviates by prefix.
For pn entries, the dominant prefix is "al_" as in "al_pn-2"; "ak_pn-2" deviates by using the rare prefix "ak_".
The pn series uses numeric suffixes, sometimes with a trailing letter as in "3b" and "5c"; "al_pn-xb" has a nonnumeric base and breaks the pattern.
For pn entries, the dominant prefix is "al_"; "ax_pn-2" uses the rare prefix "ax_" instead of matching "al_pn-2".
The ami series is consistently "ami" as in "al_ami-7a" and "al_ami-1"; "al_amx-7a" misspells the stem.
"al_amx-1" breaks the established "al_ami-*" stem seen in "al_ami-1" to "al_ami-8a".
The stem "scip" is dominant in values like "al_scip-inf-2"; "al_sxip-inf-2" has a one-character misspelling.

The ami stem is established by many values like "al_ami-1"; "al_axi-1" has an internal misspelling of the stem.
Most pn values use the pattern "al_pn-<suffix>"; "al_pnx6" is missing the separator characters found in "al_pn-6".
Most pn values use the pattern "al_pn-<suffix>"; "al_pnx2" is missing the separator characters found in "al_pn-2".
